content.full_text:
BRAD SHERMAN
MEMBER
STATE BOARD OF EQUALIZATION

March 18, 1991

Ms. Susan Bobrow, Esq.
Counsel, Legal Division
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 "J" Street
Sacramento, CA 95814

Dear Ms. Bobrow:

Thank you for speaking to me on the telephone March 14, 1991. In our conversation, I inquired whether an elected state officer's receipt from a real estate agent of a $1,500 discount on the agent's $5,500 commission on a condominium purchase transaction constitutes a reportable gift subject to the $250.00 single-source gift limitation of Government Code section 89504, effective January 1, 1991.

In our conversation, I mentioned that the agent frequently gave these discounts without regard to the public official status, or lack thereof, of the customer. I further suggested that this fact removed such a discount from the definition of "gift" in Government Code section 82028(a), which provides in pertinent part that the term "gift" "includes a rebate or discount in the price of any thing of value unless the rebate or discount is made in the regular course of business to members of the public without regard to official status." (Underlining added)

When you telephoned me at approximately 3:15 P.M. March 14, you stated that the Commission staff's telephone advice was that, "to the extent the agent's normal business practice is to provide a discount", it would not be considered a gift, either for reporting purposes or the Section 89504 $250.00 single-source limit.

Thank you for your prompt and informative response to my request for information. If you have any questions, please call.

Very truly yours,

Steven M. Kamp
Legal Counsel to Member

The image you provided is mostly blank, with very faint and mostly illegible text in the upper right corner. The majority of the content appears to be mostly white space. If there is any specific question or task related to this image that you would like assistance with, please provide more details or another image.
